meaning: adj.詹姆士一世的,黑橡木色的n.詹姆士一世时期的
examples: Several people looked over the part - Jacobean, part-Georgian building.
When they were together the Jacobean and the Victorian ages were juxtaposed.
She also sanded down the original oak floors and stained them a dark Jacobean brown.
Shakespeare's Globe is setting up a new youth company to specialise in Jacobean period drama.
It will be performed at the candle-lit Sam Wanamaker Playhouse, the Globe's new indoor Jacobean-style theatre which is still under construction.
